title: rhododendron viscosum var. glaucum
intro: Rhododendron viscosum var. glaucum, also known as the Sticky Azalea, is a deciduous shrub native to eastern North America, characterized by its sticky or glandular leaves and white or pale pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rhododendron viscosum var. glaucum include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive issues, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and decoctions, can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effectiveness and potential side effects.
